title: Context Registry — confusable workstreams + mandatory difference notes
description: TEMPLATE, not data. The GUARDRAIL that stops lookalike workstreams overwriting each other (ARCHITECTURE.md §6 layer 2; the canonical scar is the Bosch/STMicro tester confusion, §7). Mechanism only: every value below is a {company-slot} marker; the filled instance copy is `company_profile.context_registry` in a manifest.json validated against tooling/config.schema.json. Each entry = one confusable workstream: a kebab `<entity>-<workstream>` tag, its owning current-position file, and EVERY related-but-different sibling carrying a MANDATORY difference note.
x-register-before-use: REGISTER-BEFORE-USE: before writing into a workstream that has any confusable sibling, an agent reads its entry here and the sibling difference notes FIRST. A new workstream that forks from an existing one MUST be registered (new tag + reciprocal difference note on the sibling) before content lands — an unregistered fork is the drift the sensor flags. Define each pairing's difference on BOTH siblings (the relationship is reciprocal).

tag.description: {context-tag} — kebab-case <entity>-<workstream>, e.g. '{entity}-{workstream}'. Matches the `context:` frontmatter value content nodes in this workstream carry.

what.description: {context-what} — one-line description of the workstream (scope, key metric/path that distinguishes it from its siblings).

owner.description: {owner-path} — forward-slash root-relative path to the current-position / CLAUDE.md file that OWNS this context, or null if not yet created.

related.description: Sibling contexts that are EASILY CONFUSED with this one. Each carries the load-bearing difference note. Omit/empty only when the workstream has no confusable sibling.

tag.description: {sibling-tag} — the related context's tag (should itself be a registered entry).

difference.description: {difference-note} — MANDATORY. WHY this sibling must never bleed into the context: the one fact (beam path, metric, customer-vs-product line, entity) that separates them. The guardrail's whole value lives in this note.

x-examples.description: NOT part of the registry — illustration only. The registry value IS the array; an instance fills the array directly (this `x-examples` key is dropped).

related.difference: {difference-note} — never port {sibling}'s terms into this context.
